steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes Greenidge-Sabral's goal: avoid harsh synthetic dyes. The second describes her action: experimenting with natural alternatives. The transition must show that her action is a response to her goal.

steps.title: Select the purpose transition
steps.content: ”To that end” captures the purpose relationship — the action is taken to achieve the goal. The other three either downplay the connection, invent a contrast, or suggest a correction instead. That matches choice B — B is correct.
